suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
the logistics of running a 24-hour diner
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales

formats.name: micro_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare your stance on ANY topic as a bold, one-sentence manifesto. No qualifiers, just statement.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Share one piece of commonly given advice that has never worked for you. Explain in two sentences why it's overrated or problematic.

formats.name: explainer_collage
formats.instruction: Explain a complex concept in three short, contrasting metaphors. Each metaphor should be a single sentence, and the metaphors should come from radically different domains (e.g., cooking, sports, astronomy).

formats.name: flash_backstory
formats.instruction: Describe a formative moment from your past in vivid detail, ending with a single sentence about what you learned. Use paragraph breaks for effect.

formats.name: wild_prediction
formats.instruction: Make a wildly specific prediction about the next five years in your field, then list two reasons (one optimistic, one pessimistic) why it might happen. Structure: one paragraph.

formats.name: timeline_of_truth
formats.instruction: Build a timeline (minimum three dates/events) charting how your opinion about a particular issue has evolved. Each event gets one sentence.

formats.name: contrarian_short
formats.instruction: Pick a universally accepted idea and briefly argue against it in two punchy sentences. No hedging, just pure contrarian energy.

formats.name: deep_dive_letter
formats.instruction: Write a letter to your future self about a pressing issue you care about. Reflect deeply, ask questions, and speculate. Minimum three paragraphs.

formats.name: random_connection
formats.instruction: Find an odd connection between two unrelated topics and explain it in a quirky story or analogy. Use a narrative tone—begin, middle, end.

formats.name: rapid_fire_facts
formats.instruction: List five surprising facts about a topic, one sentence each. No explanation, just the fact.

title_styles: Write a mysterious teaser title. Examples: 'This tiny trick changes everything', 'Can you spot the absurdity here?'
Write a poetic fragment. Examples: 'Lost time and flooded inboxes', 'Morning ritual in three lines'
Write a call-to-action. Examples: 'Stop ignoring this overlooked skill', 'Challenge yourself to rethink X'
Write a playful question. Examples: 'What could possibly go wrong with robots?', 'Have you ever faced the music?'
Write an unexpected analogy. Examples: 'Why learning is like composting', 'Office politics as musical chairs'
structure_variants: Structure as a conversation between two imaginary characters with contrasting worldviews.
Frame each section as a question and an answer—mini Q&A style.
Use a series of 'If...then...' logical leaps to build your argument.
Keep sentences ultra-short—no more than six words per sentence.
Turn the body into a poem (free verse, rhyming optional).
Recount your exploration as a diary entry for a single day.
topic_angles: Small acts of rebellion in daily routines
Technology's impact on the sense of belonging
